title: Candidature de la Micronésie au sein de l'OIE

pages.text:
DEPARTMENT OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS

of the

FEDERATED STATES OF MICRONESIA

P.O. Box P.S.123

Palikir. Pohnpei FM 96941

Telephone: (691) 320-2641/2613 Fax: (691) 320-2933

June 26, 2008

Dear Chairman Vallat,

RE: Application for World organisation for animal health Membership

The Federated States of Micronesia (FSM) presents its complements to the

French Ministry of Foreign Affairs and has the honor to inform that the Govemment of the F.S.M is interested in the World Organization for Animal Health (OIE) membership.

The Federated States of Micronesia is a developing country located in the Northern part of the Pacific Ocean. It consists of about 607 small islands (only 65 are inhabited) with a total land area of about 270.8 square miles and an exclusive economic zone of over 1 million square miles. The FSM is a federation of four States namely; Chuuk, Kosrae,

Pohnpei and Yap with a population of about 110 000 people and a GDP of about USD 212 million.

The FSM became a party to the ACP-EU partnership agreement also known as the Cotonou Agreement 2000. Through this relation, the FSM had access to the EU markets.

FSM exports live clams into a number of European countries. The FSM has recently been informed about the European Communities Draft Regulation on certain animal health requirement for placing on the market and import of aquaculture animals and products thereof" which requires FSM to become an OIE member for its clams exports to continue accessing the European market after August 2008, when the

Regulation is expected to enter into force.

Even though FSM exports to the EU are

minimal at the moment, they play a vital role in supporting the livelihood of small island communities. It is in this context that FSM is requesting information on OIE membership and obligations of members.

We appreciate any information you can provide on this matter to assist us in our review

Thank you for your assistance

Secretary
